cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why Babbage failed to continue his research work in 1828. The options are A through D. First, I need to look at the paragraph provided and find the part that talks about 1828. Let me read through it again. The third paragraph says: "Then his health broke down, and he had to take a long holiday abroad. When he returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools. For one year no work was done." So when he returned in 1828, he was out of resources (money?), unpaid bills, and his co-workers left. The question is about why he couldn't continue research then. The options are A (poor health), B (no money), C (co-workers argued), D (spent money on bills). The paragraph mentions that when he returned in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. That's probably B (he almost ran out of money). Also, the health breakdown was before 1828, during the four years in London, leading to his holiday. So his health had already broken down before 1828.
